Title: First Access to the Application
Preconditions: User has not accessed the application before.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. The application displays the first access screen.
3. User clicks on the "Register" or "Login" button.
Expected Result: The user is directed to the registration or login screen.

Title: Return to the Application
Preconditions: User has already accessed the application.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. The application displays the home screen.
Expected Result: The user is returned to the home screen.

Title: Start a Trip
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Start a Trip" button on the home screen.
2. The application transitions to the "Trip in Progress" state.
Expected Result: The user is able to start a trip.

Title: Change Section
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Change Section" button on the home screen.
2. The application transitions to the "Menu Horizontal" state.
Expected Result: The user is able to change the section.

Title: Trip Completion
Test Steps:
1. User completes the trip.
2. The application transitions to the "Trip Completed" state.
Expected Result: The trip is completed, and the emissions are calculated.

Title: Emission Calculation
Test Steps: 1. The application calculates the emissions based on the completed trip.

Title: View Weekly Report
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Statistics" button on the home screen.
2. User clicks on the "View Weekly Report" button.

Title: Change Section
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Change Section" button.
2. The application transitions to the "Menu Horizontal" state.
Expected Result: The user is able to change the section.

Title: View Personal Data
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Profile" button on the home screen.
2. User clicks on the "View Personal Data" button.
Expected Result: The user's personal data is displayed.

Title: Logout
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Profile" button on the home screen.
2. User clicks on the "Logout" button.
Expected Result: The user is logged out, and the first access screen is displayed.

Title: Cancel Logout
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Profile" button on the home screen.
2. User clicks on the "Cancel" button instead of the "Logout" button.
Expected Result: The user remains logged in, and the profile screen is displayed.
